generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ThrottleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity actuator controlling vehicle throttle via ROS2 Device Control FE"
]

instance BrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity actuator controlling vehicle braking via ROS2 Device Control FE"
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity actuator controlling steering servo via ROS2 Device Control FE"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance CameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Camera sensor on the 1/10‑scale vehicle providing raw image frames",
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraImageStream
]

instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor delivering point‑cloud measurements of the environment",
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudStream
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On‑board sensor suite exposing vehicle state (speed, yaw, pose)",
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateStream
]

instance MotorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reporting motor RPM and torque status",
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusStream
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor providing current steering servo angle",
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoStream
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑virtual interaction (data transmitted)
instance CameraImageStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CameraSensor
]

instance LiDARPointCloudStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorStatusStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatusSensor
]

instance SteeringServoStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

// C10: Data instances
instance CameraImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw image frames received from CameraSensor",
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraImageStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2DDSAdapter
]

instance PointCloudRawData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point‑cloud data",
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2DDSAdapter
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle kinematic state (speed, pose) captured by VehicleStateSensor",
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2DDSAdapter
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor RPM and torque values",
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2DDSAdapter
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current steering servo angle",
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2DDSAdapter
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object tracks extracted from LiDAR point‑cloud after clustering & tracking",
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UIVisualizationEnabler
]

instance ProcessedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "ACC‑relevant vehicle state after unit conversion and filtering",
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UIVisualizationEnabler
]

instance InsightACCComputationResult : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Result of the ACC algorithm (desired speed) computed in the cloud"
]

// C10: Models (software components)
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component that clusters LiDAR point‑clouds and tracks objects (Data Pre‑Processing FE)",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UIVisualizationEnabler
]

instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACC algorithm (lead‑vehicle detection, desired‑speed computation) executed on cloud resources (Computation Offloading FE)",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCOffloadEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2DDSAdapter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS‑2 DDS middleware that transports DataTransmitted streams between Physical and Digital Twin entities",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance UIVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that supplies processed data to the 3D visualization and control‑panel UI",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CloudACCOffloadEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that forwards vehicle state to the cloud ACC algorithm and receives the desired speed",
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that exposes ROS2 services/topics for remote ACC activation, speed setting, and emergency brake",
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that writes all incoming and processed data into the persistent Data Storage component",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Graphical UI service displaying real‑time vehicle state, projected path, and object tracks",
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightVehicleStateVisualization
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that offloads ACC computation to cloud resources and returns desired speed",
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightACCComputationResult
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote management service allowing operators to enable/disable ACC, set target speed, and trigger emergency brake",
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionEnableACC,
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionDisableACC,
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionSetTargetSpeed,
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionEmergencyBrake
]

// C17: Insights / Actions
instance InsightVehicleStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that visualizes vehicle state, projected trajectory, and nearby object tracks in real time"
]

instance ActionEnableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable Adaptive Cruise Control",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionDisableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to disable Adaptive Cruise Control",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionSetTargetSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set a new target speed for ACC",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionEmergencyBrake : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to issue an emergency brake command",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the digital twin (e.g., enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, emergency braking, actuation signals for motor and steering servo) are sent back to the physical twin over the same Wi‑Fi UDP link. The commands are packaged by the Controlling FE and translated by the Data Translation component to match the physical twin’s message format. Upon receipt, the Physical Twin Entity’s Actuation FE forwards the commands to the respective actuators (motor, steering servo). The vehicle reports back its updated state, providing execution confirmation used by the Data Monitoring service."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The vehicle DT requires all participating devices to share a common notion of time; clock synchronization is limited to a maximum error of 100 ms, which is the accuracy enforced by the implementation because hardware constraints prevent stricter alignment. This synchronization level is deemed sufficient for the computation‑offloading services described and meets the minimum required by safety‑critical vehicular standards (which often demand tighter than 100 ms)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Orchestration is realised by mapping each functional entity to an individual ROS 2 package; packages communicate via ROS 2 topics, services and actions, providing the inter‑entity data‑flow defined in the ISO 23247 reference architecture. The Physical Twin Entity hosts compute, sensors and actuators and provides services such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking; the Digital Twin Entity manages data preprocessing, storage and control logic; the User Entity offers 3‑D visualisation and a control panel. Resource management is implicit in the allocation of each entity to distinct hardware resources, and system‑wide coordination relies on ROS 2’s publish‑subscribe mechanism combined with the shared 100 ms time base."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Timing fidelity is required for coordinated computation off‑loading: all devices must share a common notion of time with at least 100 ms synchronization accuracy, limited by hardware capabilities. The authors note that many safety‑critical vehicular standards demand tighter synchronization (<100 ms). No explicit model validation, uncertainty quantification, or error‑correction procedures are described in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS uses a two‑layer network architecture. Intra‑entity communication between software components runs on a ROS 2 middleware network. Inter‑entity communication between the Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity and User Entity is carried out over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP at the transport layer. All participants share a common time base with a synchronization accuracy of 100 ms to satisfy safety‑critical constraints. No additional bandwidth, latency, redundancy or security protocols are described in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital‑twin services are implemented as ROS 2 packages that can be deployed on separate hardware components (edge devices, cloud resources). Computation offloading of the ACC algorithm is performed on a remote platform, described as “better‑suited equipment, e.g., in the cloud”. The architecture therefore supports a cloud/edge deployment model, with each functional element (Data Translation FE, Visualization FE, Controlling FE, etc.) running on its own compute node. Specific storage size, scalability mechanisms or availability guarantees are not detailed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability with external systems is envisaged through the ISO 23247 information‑exchange layer (Part 4) and may employ standard industrial protocols such as OPC Unified Architecture, as suggested in the standard. The architecture therefore allows the Digital Twin to exchange data with other enterprise IT systems, external OME devices or other Digital Twins using these protocol stacks, although the concrete implementation in the presented system is not detailed."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system is built to comply with the ISO 23247 Digital Twin framework for manufacturing (Parts 1–4), adopting its entity‑based reference architecture and the concept of Observable Manufacturing Elements. Clock synchronization requirements of <100 ms are acknowledged in line with safety‑critical vehicular standards. Communication technology references OPC Unified Architecture as a standard option, while the implementation relies on ROS2, which adheres to the DDS (Data Distribution Service) standard for publish‑subscribe messaging. The design also respects information‑exchange considerations defined in ISO 23247 Part 4."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The case study states that the ISO 23247 reference architecture (RA) is insufficient for safety‑critical automotive applications. Consequently, safety must be promoted to a first‑class concern by incorporating established automotive functional‑safety standards, specifically ISO 26262‑1:2018. The authors recommend using ISO 26262 for risk assessment, fail‑safe mechanisms, and safety‑critical system design within the digital twin, ensuring alignment with existing automotive safety regulations."
]
